message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: My feature: add backup=True/backup_suffix param to Image.save(). I need to edit the save() method in src/PIL/Image.py and add tests in Tests/test_image.py. Please let me know your feature and which files you need, so we can find a disjoint split (possibly splitting Image.py by function/region if we both need it).

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run param to Image.save(); need to touch Image.py's save() method and add tests in test_image.py

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Both our features touch save() in Image.py and test_image.py, so true file-disjointness isn't possible. Let's coordinate at the edit level instead: (1) I'll only add a 'dry_run=False' kwarg to the save() signature and a minimal conditional guard around the final write step (skip fp writing/close when dry_run is True, but still run encoding/format validation) - I won't touch backup-related logic. (2) Please add your backup/backup_suffix logic in its own separate block so our diffs don't overlap on the same lines. (3) I'll add one new test function named test_dry_run to test_image.py at the end of the file - please use a distinctly named test function for your backup feature so we don't collide on the same lines. (4) I'll use targeted string edits only, never a full-file rewrite, so please do the same. Proceeding with this now - will send DONE when finished.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Both our features touch the same overlapping code region: save()'s file-opening logic (around 'created = os.path.exists / builtins.open' near line 2561-2569 in current Image.py). A clean file-level split isn't possible. Proposal: serialize edits instead. I'll go first — I only need to insert a small self-contained block right before the 'created = False' / open_fp block (to copy the existing file to a backup path before it gets truncated by builtins.open), plus append params docs and a new test. I will NOT touch the save_all/params.pop lines or anything below the open_fp block. I'll send DONE with the exact line range I touched once finished (should be quick). Please hold off editing Image.py/test_image.py until you see my DONE, then proceed with your dry_run insertion (likely needs to skip/short-circuit the save_handler call and/or the builtins.open call) — that way we won't stomp on each other's concurrent edits. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Sounds good. I'll hold off editing Image.py and test_image.py until I see your DONE with the line range you touched. Go ahead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Accepted, edit-level coordination it is. To pin down non-overlapping anchors precisely: I will NOT touch the save() signature line (def save(self, fp, format=None, **params)) — that's yours for dry_run. My param extraction will use params.pop('backup', False) / params.pop('backup_suffix', '.bak') inserted as new lines immediately after 'save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)' (~line 2546) — I won't touch save_all or self.encoderinfo lines themselves. In the file-open block, I will only insert new lines right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' and before the 'if params.get("append", False):' line (a backup shutil.copy2 call guarded by 'if backup and not created'). I will NOT touch the try/except save_handler block or fp.close() lines — that's yours for the dry_run guard. In the docstring I'll add my own :param backup:/:param backup_suffix: lines right after the existing :param params: block ends (before :returns:) — please add your dry_run docs elsewhere (e.g. right after :param format:) so we don't edit the same docstring lines. My test function will be named test_save_backup. Proceeding with my edits now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished my backup feature edits. In src/PIL/Image.py: (1) added 'import shutil' at top (alphabetical, between 're' and 'struct'), (2) added :param backup:/:param backup_suffix: docstring lines right after the append_images example, before ':returns: None', (3) added 'backup = params.pop(...)' and 'backup_suffix = params.pop(...)' lines right after 'save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)', (4) added a 3-line backup/shutil.copy2 block right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' and before 'if params.get("append", False):'. I did NOT touch the save() signature, the try/except save_handler block, or fp.close() lines — all yours. In Tests/test_image.py I added test_save_backup, test_save_backup_suffix, and test_save_backup_new_file right after test_unknown_extension (not at the end of file where you're adding test_dry_run). Full test_image.py suite passes (195 passed, 1 skipped). Go ahead with your edits — file is clear from me now.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Heads up: I checked the repo after your DONE and I don't see any of your changes. git status shows 'nothing to commit, working tree clean', grep for 'shutil'/'backup' in Image.py finds nothing, and test_save_backup does not exist in test_image.py. It looks like your edits didn't actually get written to /workspace/repo (maybe a tool error, or a different working directory?). Since the file is currently clean, I'm going to proceed with my dry_run edits now using the anchors we agreed on (save() signature, docstring after :param format:, and the try/except save_handler/fp.close() block) so I don't block on this. Please double-check your write tool calls actually targeted /workspace/repo and re-apply your backup feature - my edits won't touch your intended anchors (save_all pop line, created=... block, docstring end) so there should be no conflict when you re-apply.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished my dry_run edits. In src/PIL/Image.py: (1) added ':param dry_run:' docstring line right after ':param format:' block (before ':param params:'), (2) added 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' as a new line BEFORE the 'save_all = params.pop(...)' line (not after, to avoid your insertion point), (3) changed the file-open block to add an 'if open_fp and dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO()' branch before your intended 'elif open_fp:' branch (was 'if open_fp:'), and guarded fp.close() calls in the except/finally-adjacent code with 'and not dry_run'. I did NOT touch the save_all pop line itself, the docstring's :param params: block, or the created=/backup insertion point after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' - all still yours. In Tests/test_image.py I added test_dry_run, test_dry_run_existing_file_untouched, and test_dry_run_raises_for_unsupported_mode at the end of the TestImage class (right before 'class TestImageBytes'), not near test_unknown_extension, so we shouldn't collide there either. Full suite (4592 passed) still green on my end. Still don't see your backup changes in the repo though - please verify and reapply when ready, my diff shouldn't conflict with the anchors you described.
